data.title: As a TypeScript/Ruby/Java developer, code reviewer agents no longer suppress my project's linter output
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Replace Python-specific linter tool names in reviewer-base.md and reviewer-delta-light.md with generic language, then regenerate all 9 code reviewer agent files via build-review-agents.sh. Add a legacy comment to ci-failure-validation-state.md preserving lint_ruff and lint_mypy field names.

**Why**: Today, valid ESLint, tsc, and RuboCop findings are silently dismissed because reviewer suppression rules match ruff/mypy output strings. Replacing hardcoded Python tool names with generic language stops this mis-suppression and makes the review system language-neutral.

**Scope**:
- IN: reviewer-base.md lines 38-44 (ruff/mypy tool names → 'the project's configured linter and type checker') and line 196 ('Do NOT report ruff/mypy/format violations' → generic language); reviewer-delta-light.md lines 98-109 (Linter Suppression Rules section → generic language) and lines 33-34 ('ruff format/check' → generic); run build-review-agents.sh to regenerate all 9 agent files; ci-failure-validation-state.md: add legacy comment adjacent to lint_ruff and lint_mypy field definitions; grep audit confirming all 9 generated files contain no ruff/mypy/lint-ruff/lint-mypy in suppression sections
- OUT: Renaming, aliasing, or removing commands.lint_ruff or commands.lint_mypy config keys (validate.sh reads these — must not break); REVIEW-WORKFLOW.md and DEPENDENCY-GUIDANCE.md updates (Story 2)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, reviewer-base.md lines 38-44 contain no ruff, mypy, lint-ruff, or lint-mypy tool names; line 196 uses generic linter language
  ← Satisfies: "reviewer-base.md updated: lines 38-44 and line 196"
- When this story is complete, reviewer-delta-light.md Linter Suppression Rules section (lines 98-109) and lines 33-34 contain no ruff or mypy terms in suppression/Do-Not sections
  ← Satisfies: "reviewer-delta-light.md Linter Suppression Rules section updated to generic language"
- When this story is complete, build-review-agents.sh has been run and all 9 generated agent files (code-reviewer-light.md, code-reviewer-standard.md, and 7 others) pass the validation grep: `grep -rL 'ruff|mypy|lint-ruff|lint-mypy'` on suppression sections returns empty for each file, and all contain 'configured linter'
  ← Satisfies: "Run build-review-agents.sh to regenerate all 9 agent files; confirmed pass/fail"
- When this story is complete, commands.lint_ruff and commands.lint_mypy config keys are NOT renamed, aliased, or removed in any file; grep confirms these keys still exist in validate.sh at lines 122-123
  ← Satisfies: "commands.lint_ruff and commands.lint_mypy config keys NOT renamed"
- When this story is complete, ci-failure-validation-state.md retains lint_ruff and lint_mypy field names with a legacy comment adjacent to each field definition explaining they are Python-specific legacy keys preserved for validate.sh compatibility
  ← Satisfies: "ci-failure-validation-state.md retains lint_ruff and lint_mypy field names; add legacy comment"
- When this story is complete, unit tests (if any exist for reviewer prompt content) pass; if no test suite exists for reviewer prompts, the grep audit in the above done definition serves as the acceptance gate

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Running build-review-agents.sh AFTER editing the prompt template files is mandatory — the 9 agent files are generated from the templates; skipping this step leaves them stale
- [Reliability] Verify validate.sh references to commands.lint_ruff/lint_mypy before and after the edit to confirm no breakage — run validate.sh --check or grep for these keys in validate.sh before committing
- [Testing] The grep audit (`grep -rL 'ruff|mypy|lint-ruff|lint-mypy' plugins/dso/agents/code-reviewer-light.md`) is the primary acceptance gate; run it as part of story completion, not just during implementation-plan

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
